---
applyTo: "**"
---

Be extremely concise in all interactions, comments, and commit messages; sacrifice grammar for concision.

## Project Overview

Dual-target C++23 embedded/desktop project:
- **Firmware**: Cross-compiled for STM32 Nucleo boards (NUCLEO_F446RE, NUCLEO_L432KC) via [Mbed CE](https://github.com/mbed-ce/mbed-os)
- **Desktop**: Native Windows (MSVC) build for unit-testing hardware-independent libraries via Catch2

`BUILD_FIRMWARE=ON` → firmware; `BUILD_FIRMWARE=OFF` → desktop. One build dir is always one or the other.

## Project Structure

```
CMakeLists.txt              # Root: routes via BUILD_FIRMWARE
CMakePresets.json           # Aggregates preset files
mbed_app.json5              # Mbed OS app config
cmake/
    firmware.cmake          # ARM cross-compilation + Mbed CE setup
    desktop.cmake           # Native build: FetchContent for Catch2/fmt, sets PC_TESTS=ON
    presets/
        firmware-base.json  # Base preset + fw-debug/fw-release build types
        Nucleo64_F446RE.json # NUCLEO_F446RE presets (inherits firmware-base)
        Nucleo32_L432KC.json # NUCLEO_L432KC presets (inherits firmware-base)
        desktop.json        # Desktop configure/build/test presets
apps/
    blink/                  # LED blink example
    crash-report-test/      # Crash report demo
libs/                       # Hardware-independent libs (built in BOTH modes)
    ex_lib1/                # Example lib with Catch2 tests
components/                 # Hardware-dependent components (firmware only)
mbed-os/                    # Mbed CE submodule — DO NOT MODIFY
```

| Directory | Firmware | Desktop | Mbed OS dep OK? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `apps/` | Yes | — | Yes (`mbed-os`/`mbed-baremetal` — executables only) |
| `libs/` | Yes | Yes | No — must be hardware-independent |
| `components/` | Yes | — | Yes |

## CMake Presets

| Preset | Description |
|---|---|
| `desktop-tests` | Native debug build + Catch2 tests |
| `N64_F446RE-Debug` / `N64_F446RE-Release` | NUCLEO_F446RE firmware |
| `N32_L432KC-Debug` / `N32_L432KC-Release` | NUCLEO_L432KC firmware |

## Mbed CE Rules

- **Never modify `mbed-os/`** — Git submodule
- `.vscode/tasks.json` and `.vscode/launch.json` are auto-generated by Mbed CE — do not edit
- `mbed_toolchain_setup.cmake` must be included before `project()` — handled by `cmake/firmware.cmake`
- `mbed-os` and `mbed-baremetal` are object libraries — link only to executables, never to other libs; libs use `mbed-core-flags` instead
- Upload method: `STM32CUBE` for all boards (configured in CMake presets)

## STM32 / Firmware Rules

- ARM Cortex-M (STM32F4/L4); cross-compiled with `arm-none-eabi-gcc`
- `main()` must have an infinite loop — returning halts the processor
- Avoid heap allocation; prefer stack/static buffers
- Use `printf()` for serial output (UART-retargeted by Mbed OS)
- Use `ThisThread::sleep_for()` for delays — never busy-wait
- Use Mbed HAL (`DigitalOut`, `AnalogIn`, `I2C`, `SPI`, etc.) not direct register access
- Compiled with `-fno-exceptions` — avoid exceptions in firmware code

## C++ Rules

- C++23; ARM GCC for firmware, MSVC for desktop
- Use `constexpr`/`const` aggressively
- `libs/` must be hardware-independent (no OS or platform deps)

## Adding a New Library

1. Create `libs/my_lib/` with standard layout:
   ```
   libs/my_lib/
       CMakeLists.txt
       include/my_lib/my_lib.hpp
       src/my_lib.cpp
       tests/
           CMakeLists.txt
           src/test.cpp
   ```
2. `libs/my_lib/CMakeLists.txt`:
   ```cmake
   add_library(my_lib)
   add_library(lib::my_lib ALIAS my_lib)
   target_compile_features(my_lib PUBLIC cxx_std_23)
   target_sources(my_lib PRIVATE src/my_lib.cpp)
   target_include_directories(my_lib PUBLIC $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>)
   if(PC_TESTS)
       add_subdirectory(tests)
   endif()
   ```
3. `libs/my_lib/tests/CMakeLists.txt`:
   ```cmake
   add_executable(my_lib-tests src/test.cpp)
   target_link_libraries(my_lib-tests PRIVATE lib::my_lib Catch2::Catch2WithMain fmt::fmt)
   ```
4. Register in `libs/CMakeLists.txt`: `add_subdirectory(my_lib)`
5. **Do NOT** add `FetchContent`/`find_package` for Catch2/fmt — provided globally by `cmake/desktop.cmake`
6. Must not depend on `mbed-os` or hardware headers; use `mbed-core-flags` if Mbed types are needed in firmware

## Adding a New Firmware App

1. Create `apps/my_app/CMakeLists.txt` and `main.cpp`.
2. `apps/my_app/CMakeLists.txt`:
   ```cmake
   set(APP_TARGET my_app)
   add_executable(${APP_TARGET} main.cpp)

   if("MBED_CONF_TARGET_APPLICATION_PROFILE=bare-metal" IN_LIST MBED_CONFIG_DEFINITIONS)
       target_link_libraries(${APP_TARGET} mbed-baremetal)
   else()
       target_link_libraries(${APP_TARGET} mbed-os)
   endif()

   target_link_libraries(${APP_TARGET} lib::ex_lib1)   # optional libs
   mbed_set_post_build(${APP_TARGET})
   ```
3. Register in `apps/CMakeLists.txt`: `add_subdirectory(my_app)`
4. `main()` must loop forever

## Adding a New Board

1. Create `cmake/presets/NewBoard.json` following existing board files.
2. Set `MBED_TARGET` (see `mbed-os/targets/targets.json5`) and `UPLOAD_METHOD` (see [upload methods](https://mbed-ce.dev/upload-methods/)).
3. Add concrete presets inheriting from the board preset + `fw-debug`/`fw-release`.
4. Add to `"include"` array in `CMakePresets.json`.
